fixes.issue_summary: Invoiced Sales tab not appearing in Report Visibility admin page
fixes.root_cause: The frontend ReportVisibility.jsx has its own hardcoded REPORT_REGISTRY separate from the backend. The 'invoiced-sales' tab was added to the backend REPORT_REGISTRY in report_visibility.py but was never added to the frontend's copy. The frontend renders visibility toggles from its local registry, not the backend's.
fixes.solution: Added 'invoiced-sales': { label: 'Invoiced Sales' } to the frontend REPORT_REGISTRY in reporting-frontend/src/components/admin/ReportVisibility.jsx, positioned between 'sales' and 'sales-breakdown'.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — the Report Visibility admin page is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL: There are THREE registries that must stay in sync when adding new tabs: (1) Backend NAVIGATION_CONFIG in rbac_config.py, (2) Backend REPORT_REGISTRY in report_visibility.py, (3) Frontend REPORT_REGISTRY in ReportVisibility.jsx. Missing any one of these causes the tab to be invisible in different contexts.

fixes.issue_summary: Invoiced Sales tab not appearing on the Sales Dashboard page even though Report Visibility toggle was enabled
fixes.root_cause: The 'invoiced-sales' tab was missing from the NAVIGATION_CONFIG in rbac_config.py. The frontend Dashboard.jsx filters visible tabs using dashboardTabs from getAccessibleTabs(user, 'dashboard'), which reads from user.navigation.dashboard.tabs. Since the backend NAVIGATION_CONFIG didn't include 'invoiced-sales', it was never sent in the navigation response.
fixes.solution: Added 'invoiced-sales': {'label': 'Invoiced Sales', 'resource': 'dashboard'} to the NAVIGATION_CONFIG dashboard tabs section in rbac_config.py.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — NAVIGATION_CONFIG is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: The NAVIGATION_CONFIG controls which tabs appear in the user's navigation response. If a tab is in REPORT_REGISTRY but not NAVIGATION_CONFIG, it will appear in Report Visibility admin but not on the actual dashboard page.

fixes.issue_summary: Sales Manager role (Mike Cooper) seeing Accounting, Customers, QBR, My Commissions, Rep Comp Admin in sidebar instead of just Sales
fixes.root_cause: The Sales Manager role in ROLE_PERMISSIONS had too many resources: ['dashboard', 'accounting_commissions', 'my_commissions', 'manage_rep_comp', 'minitrac', 'qbr', 'customer_churn']. Each resource grants access to a sidebar page.
fixes.solution: Restricted Sales Manager resources to only ['dashboard'] so they only see the Sales page in the sidebar.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — RBAC roles are universal
fixes.lessons_learned: ROLE_PERMISSIONS resources directly control sidebar navigation. Each resource maps to a NAVIGATION_CONFIG entry. To restrict a role to specific pages, only include the corresponding resources.

fixes.issue_summary: Invoiced Sales showing $0.00 for IPS (Industrial Parts and Service) while Bennett showed correct data
fixes.root_cause: The invoiced-summary and invoiced-details endpoints had hardcoded SaleCode values ('LINDE', 'NEWEQ', 'USEDEQ', 'ALLIED', etc.) that were specific to Bennett's Softbase configuration. IPS uses completely different SaleCode values ('C1', 'I4', 'V1', 'IR', etc.). Additionally, the same Dept numbers mean different things across tenants: Dept 20 = 'Used Equipment' at Bennett but 'Allied' at IPS.
fixes.solution: Rewrote both endpoints to dynamically query the Dept table first, then categorize departments by Title keywords: 'new' + 'equip' = New Equipment, 'used' + 'equip' = Used Equipment, 'allied' = Allied Equipment. This works for any tenant regardless of their SaleCode or Dept number configuration.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Fix is universal — works for all current and future tenants automatically
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL MULTI-TENANT RULE: NEVER hardcode SaleCodes or Dept numbers. Always use the Dept table for dynamic lookup by Title keywords. Different tenants use completely different codes and numbering systems. The Dept table's Title field is the only reliable way to categorize across tenants.

fixes.issue_summary: Sales Manager getting 403 Forbidden on Invoiced Sales tab
fixes.root_cause: The invoiced-summary and invoiced-details endpoints used @require_permission('view_commissions') decorator. The Sales Manager role only has the 'dashboard' resource which grants 'view_dashboard' permission, not 'view_commissions'. Since Invoiced Sales is a dashboard tab (not an accounting feature), it should use 'view_dashboard'.
fixes.solution: Changed both endpoints from @require_permission('view_commissions') to @require_permission('view_dashboard').
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — permission decorators are universal
fixes.lessons_learned: When adding new endpoints for a specific page/tab, the permission decorator must match the page's resource, not where the data conceptually belongs. Dashboard tabs use 'view_dashboard', not 'view_commissions' even if they show financial data.

fixes.issue_summary: Cross-tenant data pollution: Bennett users seeing IPS (Ohio) data on PM Route Planner, QBR, and Rental Service Report pages
fixes.root_cause: Cache keys for Azure SQL queries were flat strings without tenant identifiers (e.g., 'pm_report_pms_due', 'qbr_customers', 'rental_service_report'). When one org loaded a page first, the result was cached globally. When another org loaded the same page, the cache returned the first org's data instead of querying the correct tenant's database. The cache_service.cache_query() function stores results in a dict keyed by cache_key — if the key is the same for both orgs, they share data.
fixes.solution: Added get_tenant_schema() to every Azure SQL cache key so each org gets its own cache entry. Example: 'pm_report_pms_due' became f'pm_report_pms_due_{schema}' where schema is 'ben002' or 'ind004'. Fixed in pm_report.py, department_reports.py (rental_service_report), and qbr.py (qbr_customers, qbr_data).
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: CRITICAL — This was an active cross-tenant data leak. Any cached Azure SQL endpoint without tenant-scoped keys will serve Org A's data to Org B.
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL MULTI-TENANT RULE: Every cache key for Azure SQL data MUST include the tenant schema (e.g., f'report_name_{schema}'). Use get_tenant_schema() to get the schema string. PostgreSQL/Vital routes are single-tenant and exempt. When adding ANY new cached endpoint that queries Azure SQL, ALWAYS include schema in the cache key. When auditing existing code, grep for 'cache_key' and verify every Azure SQL route includes tenant scoping.

fixes.issue_summary: Service Invoice Billing Report showing invoices from all departments (sales, rentals, parts) instead of only service invoices. Reopened: user also requested exclusion of intercompany bill-to customers IPS140, IPS145, IPC140, IPC145.
fixes.root_cause: Original: No SaleDept filter — only excluded parts invoices (130%) and included everything else. Reopen: Intercompany billing customers (IPS140, IPS145, IPC140, IPC145) were appearing in the report.
fixes.solution: Original fix: Added dynamic service department resolution via Dept table keyword matching. Reopen fix: Added excluded_bill_to_customers filter from organization settings JSON. IPS org configured with excluded_bill_to_customers: ['IPS140', 'IPS145', 'IPC140', 'IPC145']. The filter adds AND i.BillTo NOT IN (...) to the SQL query when org settings contain the list.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Fix is configurable per-org via settings JSON. No impact on orgs without excluded_bill_to_customers configured.
fixes.lessons_learned: Use organization settings JSON for per-org filtering configurations (excluded_branches, excluded_bill_to_customers). This pattern allows admin-configurable exclusions without code changes. Always use parameterized SQL (NOT IN with %s placeholders) to prevent injection.

fixes.issue_summary: Sales Manager (Mike Cooper at IPS) getting HTTP 403 Forbidden when accessing Invoiced Sales report
fixes.root_cause: Dual permission system mismatch: The navigation system uses ROLE_PERMISSIONS config from rbac_config.py (which correctly grants Sales Manager access to 'dashboard' resource), but the API endpoint's @require_permission decorator checks the database role_permissions table via user.has_permission(). Many roles (including Sales Manager at IPS, role ID 32) had ZERO entries in the role_permissions table, causing all permission checks to fail even though navigation showed the tabs.
fixes.solution: Updated User.has_permission() and User.has_any_permission() in user.py to also check the ROLE_PERMISSIONS config from rbac_config.py as a fallback when database permissions are empty. The method now: (1) checks DB role_permissions first, (2) if not found, looks up the user's role name in ROLE_PERMISSIONS config, (3) resolves resources to permissions via RESOURCE_PERMISSIONS mapping. This aligns the permission check with the navigation system.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — fixes permission resolution for any role with empty DB permissions. Especially important for newly created org roles that don't get DB permissions populated.
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL RBAC INSIGHT: There are TWO permission systems that must agree: (1) ROLE_PERMISSIONS config in rbac_config.py (used for navigation/sidebar), (2) role_permissions DB table (used by @require_permission decorator). When roles are created for new orgs, the DB permissions may not get populated. The fix is to have has_permission() fall back to the config-based system. Always verify both systems when debugging 403 errors.

fixes.issue_summary: Two requests: (1) Add date range picker to Invoiced Sales report, (2) Remove CH Steel Solutions and Dynamic Storage Solutions branches from IPS report
fixes.root_cause: Feature request — the report only supported month-level selection. IPS also has branches (CH Steel, Dynamic Storage) that should not appear in their Invoiced Sales report.
fixes.solution: (1) Added Month/Custom Range toggle to frontend InvoicedSalesReport.jsx. Month mode uses month+year dropdowns. Custom Range mode shows two calendar date pickers (start/end). Backend invoiced-summary and invoiced-details endpoints now accept optional start_date/end_date query params. (2) Added excluded_branches filter from org settings JSON. IPS org configured with excluded_branches: ['3', '4']. Backend skips excluded branches when building the report.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Date range feature available to all orgs. Branch exclusion is per-org via settings JSON.
fixes.lessons_learned: When adding date range support to existing month-based reports: (1) Make start_date/end_date optional — if not provided, fall back to current month behavior. (2) Use the existing Calendar/Popover pattern from other components (e.g., APAgingReport). (3) Per-org branch exclusion should use the organization settings JSON pattern (excluded_branches array) rather than hardcoding.

fixes.issue_summary: Cash Burn report using hardcoded $189/hr labor rate instead of actual WO labor rates. Also requested helper tooltips explaining where numbers come from.
fixes.root_cause: The backend service_shop_work_orders.py had LABOR_RATE = 189 hardcoded and used it for all unbillable labor value calculations. Different work orders have different labor rates assigned via WO.LaborRate -> LaborRate.Rate.
fixes.solution: (1) Replaced hardcoded LABOR_RATE with per-WO actual rate from LaborRate table (LEFT JOIN LaborRate lr ON wo.LaborRate = lr.Code). Each WO's excess hours are now multiplied by its own rate. Summary unbillable_labor_value is the sum of all individual WO unbillable values. (2) Added Info icon tooltips to all 4 Cash Burn summary cards (Total Cash Burn WOs, Critical Alerts, Warnings, Hours at Risk) explaining data sources, formulas, and SQL tables used.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — labor rates are now per-WO for all tenants
fixes.lessons_learned: Never hardcode labor rates. Different WO types and customers may have different rate codes. Always join to the LaborRate table via WO.LaborRate = LaborRate.Code to get the actual rate.

fixes.issue_summary: After creating a support ticket via the Help Modal, the user cannot see their newly created ticket. The modal closes after showing success message.
fixes.root_cause: After successful ticket creation, the HelpWidget showed a success message for 3 seconds and then closed the entire modal (setIsOpen(false)). The user never got to see their ticket in the 'My Tickets' list because the modal was dismissed.
fixes.solution: Changed the post-creation flow: after the 2-second success message, instead of closing the modal, the widget now automatically switches to the 'My Tickets' view (setActiveView('my-tickets')) and refreshes the ticket list (fetchMyTickets()). This way the user immediately sees their new ticket.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — HelpWidget is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: After creating an entity, always show the user the result rather than dismissing the UI. Switch to the list view and refresh data so the user can confirm their action was successful.

fixes.issue_summary: Where do the Quoted Hours come from in the Cash Burn report?
fixes.root_cause: Information request — not a bug.
fixes.solution: Answered via ticket comment: Quoted Hours = WOQuote.Amount (Type='L') / (LaborRate.Rate × (1 - LaborDiscount/100)). The WOQuote table stores dollar amounts, converted to hours using the WO's labor rate. Fallback is $189/hr if no rate found.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: N/A — information only
fixes.lessons_learned: Quoted Hours derivation: WOQuote.Amount(Type=L) / effective_rate. The WOQuote table stores labor quotes as dollar amounts, not hours. The conversion to hours depends on the WO's assigned labor rate.

fixes.issue_summary: Work orders appearing multiple times in the Cash Stalled (Awaiting Invoice) report
fixes.root_cause: The Equipment table can have multiple entries for the same UnitNo (e.g., Unit 386 had both a Lamarche charger and a Linde forklift). The LEFT JOIN Equipment e ON w.UnitNo = e.UnitNo created duplicate rows — one for each Equipment record matching the UnitNo.
fixes.solution: Replaced the direct Equipment JOIN with a deduplicated subquery using ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY UnitNo ORDER BY UnitNo) as rn, then filtered to rn = 1. Applied this fix to both the Service awaiting invoice query and the Parts awaiting invoice query.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — Equipment table structure is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL: The Equipment table can have multiple rows per UnitNo (e.g., a forklift and its charger). Any query that JOINs Equipment on UnitNo MUST use ROW_NUMBER() deduplication or a subquery to avoid row multiplication. Always check for 1:N relationships when joining dimension tables.

fixes.issue_summary: Report Visibility settings not persisting for Service > All Work Orders tab (and many other tabs across sections)
fixes.root_cause: The backend REPORT_REGISTRY in report_visibility.py was severely out of sync with the NAVIGATION_CONFIG in rbac_config.py and the frontend. Service section had only 2 tabs registered (overview, work-orders) but the actual navigation had 12 tabs. When the frontend sent a toggle for 'all-work-orders', the backend silently ignored it because it wasn't in the registry. Same issue existed for Rental (had 0 of 4 tabs), Accounting (had 0 of 7 tabs), Knowledge Base (had 0 of 4 tabs), QBR (had 0 of 5 tabs), and Customers (had 0 of 3 tabs).
fixes.solution: Synced the backend REPORT_REGISTRY with NAVIGATION_CONFIG for all affected sections: Service (12 tabs), Rental (4 tabs), Accounting (7 tabs), Knowledge Base (4 tabs), QBR (5 tabs), Customers (3 tabs including new customer-profitability).
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — REPORT_REGISTRY is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL: When adding new tabs to NAVIGATION_CONFIG, ALWAYS also add them to the REPORT_REGISTRY in report_visibility.py. The REPORT_REGISTRY controls which tabs can be toggled in Report Visibility admin. If a tab is missing from the registry, visibility changes for that tab are silently dropped. Periodic sync audits between NAVIGATION_CONFIG and REPORT_REGISTRY should be performed.

fixes.issue_summary: Admin Support Tickets page requires manual refresh to see newly submitted tickets
fixes.root_cause: The SupportTicketsPage only fetched tickets on initial mount (useEffect with empty dependency array). No auto-refresh mechanism existed.
fixes.solution: Added a 15-second auto-refresh interval using setInterval in the useEffect hook. The interval calls loadData() every 15 seconds and is properly cleaned up on component unmount.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — SupportTicketsPage is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: Admin-facing pages that display user-submitted content should have auto-refresh intervals. 15 seconds is a good balance between responsiveness and API load. Always clean up intervals in useEffect return function.

fixes.issue_summary: Enhancement: Move Customer Profitability to Customers page (all sales codes) and make Service page version show only service sales codes
fixes.root_cause: Feature request — Customer Profitability on the Service page was showing all sales codes, but the Service Manager only needs to see service-related profitability.
fixes.solution: (1) Added 'department' query parameter to the customer-profitability backend endpoint. When department='service', filters InvoiceReg by SaleCode IN ('SVE', 'SVC', 'SH', 'PM') and WO by Type IN ('S', 'SH', 'PM'). When department='parts', filters by parts codes. Default 'all' shows everything. (2) Updated CustomerProfitability.jsx to accept a 'department' prop. (3) Service page now passes department='service'. (4) Added Customer Profitability tab to CustomersPage.jsx with department='all'. (5) Updated NAVIGATION_CONFIG and REPORT_REGISTRY with the new customer-profitability tab under customer-churn section.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — new tab and filter available universally
fixes.lessons_learned: When a report needs to appear in multiple contexts with different filters, add a filter parameter to the backend and a prop to the frontend component. Don't duplicate the component — reuse it with different props. Always update all three registries (NAVIGATION_CONFIG, REPORT_REGISTRY backend, REPORT_REGISTRY frontend) when adding new tabs.

fixes.issue_summary: Where does the Series field come from in the Cost per Hour report?
fixes.root_cause: Information request — not a bug.
fixes.solution: Answered via ticket comment: Series comes from Equipment table. Primary source: Equipment.ModelGroup. Fallback derivation from Equipment.Model: (1) Before first hyphen, (2) Before first space, (3) First 4 chars if >4, (4) Full Model value. Equipment joined via InvoiceReg.SerialNo = Equipment.SerialNo.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: N/A — information only
fixes.lessons_learned: Series field derivation: Equipment.ModelGroup (primary) -> Equipment.Model parsing (fallback). The Model parsing logic extracts a prefix using hyphens, spaces, or character count as delimiters.

fixes.root_cause: Hardcoded SaleCode filters ('SVE','SVC','SH','PM') in customer profitability endpoint did not match tenant-specific department codes. Also missing {schema}. prefix on WOLabor, WOParts, WOMisc JOIN tables.
fixes.fix: Replaced hardcoded SaleCodes with dynamic Dept table lookup querying for service-related department titles. Added {schema}. prefix to all WO sub-table references.

fixes.root_cause: Hardcoded SaleCode filters ('FMBILL','FMROAD','PM-FM','FMSHOP') were Bennett-specific and did not match other tenants' maintenance contract codes.
fixes.fix: Replaced hardcoded SaleCodes with dynamic discovery from Dept table (maintenance/guaranteed maintenance departments) and SaleCode patterns (FM%, PM-FM%). Includes fallback to original codes.

fixes.root_cause: User requested date picker - feature already existed with Calendar range picker, month dropdown, and year dropdown.
fixes.fix: No code changes needed. Resolved with documentation of existing functionality.

fixes.root_cause: User requested new Currie Service report page - feature already existed with 12 KPI cards, help tooltips, GP% trend chart, and benchmarks table.
fixes.fix: No code changes needed. Resolved with documentation of existing functionality.

fixes.root_cause: Hardcoded SaleCode filters did not match tenant-specific department codes. Missing schema prefix on WO sub-tables.
fixes.fix: Dynamic Dept table lookup + schema prefix fix

fixes.root_cause: Hardcoded Bennett-specific SaleCodes for maintenance contracts.
fixes.fix: Dynamic SaleCode discovery from Dept table
Already implemented - documented existing 12 KPI cards with tooltips

fixes.issue_summary: Cash Burn report showing wrong billable rate (base rate instead of effective rate after discount), wrong unbillable labor value, and wrong hours at risk (missing no-quote WOs)
fixes.root_cause: Three issues: (1) Billable rate displayed was the base LaborRate.Rate, ignoring WO.LaborDiscount. (2) Unbillable labor value used base rate for dollar calculations. (3) Hours at Risk only counted WOs over quoted hours, missing WOs with NO quote at all (15 of 21 WOs).
fixes.solution: (1) Added effective_rate calculation: LaborRate.Rate * (1 - LaborDiscount/100). Backend now returns effective_rate, labor_rate, and labor_discount per WO. (2) Unbillable labor value now uses effective_rate for calculations. (3) Hours at Risk now includes NO_QUOTE WOs — total = over-budget hours + no-quote hours. (4) Added Billable Rate column to table with tooltip showing base rate, discount %, and effective rate.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — effective rate calculation is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL: WOs can have a LaborDiscount field (0-100%). The effective billable rate = LaborRate.Rate × (1 - WO.LaborDiscount/100). ALWAYS use effective rate for dollar calculations, not the base rate. Also, Hours at Risk must include WOs with NO quote — these represent untracked budget exposure.

fixes.issue_summary: Confirm where Quoted Hours come from and send to John — potential process change needed
fixes.root_cause: Information request with process implications.
fixes.solution: Documented: Quoted Hours = WOQuote.Amount (Type='L') / Effective Labor Rate. Effective Rate = LaborRate.Rate × (1 - WO.LaborDiscount/100). Found 15 of 21 open WOs have no quote entered — process change needed for IPS team to enter labor quotes on all WOs.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: N/A — information and process recommendation
fixes.lessons_learned: Quoted Hours derivation: WOQuote.Amount(Type='L') / effective_rate. The WOQuote table stores labor quotes as DOLLAR amounts, not hours. The conversion to hours depends on the WO's assigned labor rate and discount. Many WOs may have no quote entered — this is a process gap that limits Cash Burn tracking effectiveness.

fixes.issue_summary: Cash Stalled showing repeating (duplicate) Work Orders
fixes.root_cause: Equipment table JOINs can produce duplicate WO rows when a UnitNo has multiple Equipment records. The ROW_NUMBER() deduplication on Equipment was already in place but edge cases in certain tenant databases could still cause duplicates.
fixes.solution: Added Python-level deduplication using a seen_wo_numbers set. If a duplicate WONo is encountered, it's skipped and a warning is logged. This is a safety net on top of the SQL-level ROW_NUMBER() deduplication.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — deduplication is a safety net for all tenants
fixes.lessons_learned: Defense in depth: Even with SQL-level deduplication (ROW_NUMBER), add Python-level deduplication as a safety net for any endpoint that displays work order lists. Log warnings when duplicates are detected to aid debugging. The Equipment table 1:N relationship with UnitNo is a recurring source of row multiplication.

fixes.issue_summary: All Work Orders tab showing total count (open + closed) instead of just open work orders
fixes.root_cause: The work-order-types endpoint had no ClosedDate or DeletionTime filter. It counted ALL WOs in the database regardless of status. Bennett showed 6,355 Service WOs when only 537 were actually open.
fixes.solution: Added ClosedDate IS NULL filter (open only) and DeletionTime IS NULL filter (not deleted). Backend now returns both open_count and total_count. Frontend shows 'X open (Y total)' for each type. Title changed to 'Open Work Orders by Type'.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — WO status filtering is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL WO STATUS FILTERS: When showing 'current' or 'active' work orders, ALWAYS apply both: (1) ClosedDate IS NULL = open, (2) DeletionTime IS NULL = not deleted. Without these filters, counts will be massively inflated with historical/closed/deleted records. Consider returning both open and total counts so users have context.

fixes.issue_summary: Document which database field the Cost per Hour is pulling from
fixes.root_cause: Enhancement request — methodology section existed but didn't list specific database fields.
fixes.solution: Expanded the Methodology section on the Cost per Hour tab with exact field names: Total Cost = SUM(InvoiceReg.PartsTaxable + PartsNonTax + LaborTaxable + LaborNonTax + MiscTaxable + MiscNonTax). Operating Hours = MAX-MIN of InvoiceReg.HourMeter. Target = Equipment.TargetCostPerHour. PM flag = Equipment.GuaranteedMaintenance. Series = Equipment.ModelGroup.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — methodology documentation is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: Cost per Hour field sources: Costs from InvoiceReg (6 cost columns: PartsTaxable, PartsNonTax, LaborTaxable, LaborNonTax, MiscTaxable, MiscNonTax). Hours from InvoiceReg.HourMeter (MAX-MIN). Target from Equipment.TargetCostPerHour. PM flag from Equipment.GuaranteedMaintenance. Series from Equipment.ModelGroup (fallback: Model parsing).

fixes.issue_summary: Recurring pattern: hardcoded SaleCodes break for non-Bennett tenants
fixes.root_cause: Bennett, IPS, and other orgs all have different SaleCode values for the same department types. Hardcoded SaleCodes have caused bugs in: Invoiced Sales (MANUAL-004), Customer Profitability (TKT-2026-0022), Maintenance Contract Profitability (TKT-2026-0021), Service Invoice Billing (TKT-2026-0006).
fixes.solution: ALWAYS use dynamic Dept table lookup: query Dept table for department titles matching keywords (e.g., 'service', 'maintenance', 'new equip'), then use the returned SaleCodes in the main query. Never hardcode SaleCode values.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: CRITICAL — this is the #1 recurring multi-tenant bug pattern
fixes.lessons_learned: GOLDEN RULE: NEVER hardcode SaleCodes or Dept numbers. The Dept table's Title field is the only reliable cross-tenant identifier. Query pattern: SELECT SaleCode FROM {schema}.Dept WHERE LOWER(Title) LIKE '%keyword%'. This has been the root cause of at least 4 separate bugs.

fixes.issue_summary: Recurring pattern: missing {schema}. prefix on WO sub-tables in SQL queries
fixes.root_cause: When writing SQL JOINs, the main WO table gets {schema}. prefix but sub-tables in subqueries (WOLabor, WOParts, WOMisc, WOQuote) are sometimes missed.
fixes.solution: Always verify ALL table references in a query have {schema}. prefix. Sub-tables to watch: WOLabor, WOParts, WOMisc, WOQuote, LaborRate, Equipment, Customer, InvoiceReg.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: CRITICAL — missing schema prefix causes queries to fail or return wrong data
fixes.lessons_learned: CHECKLIST for new SQL queries: After writing any query, scan EVERY table reference and verify it has {schema}. prefix. Common misses: sub-tables inside LEFT JOIN subqueries (SELECT ... FROM WOLabor should be SELECT ... FROM {schema}.WOLabor).

fixes.issue_summary: Ticket resolve endpoint field names
fixes.root_cause: Documentation gap — the resolve endpoint requires specific field names.
fixes.solution: POST /api/support-tickets/{id}/resolve requires: fix_summary (string) and resolution_notes (string). NOT 'resolution'. Returns 400 if neither field is provided.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: N/A — API documentation
fixes.lessons_learned: Ticket resolve endpoint: POST /api/support-tickets/{id}/resolve with JSON body containing fix_summary and resolution_notes. The field name 'resolution' does NOT work — must use fix_summary or resolution_notes.

fixes.issue_summary: Active Technicians KPI showing 10 instead of 20 for IPS — undercounting because WO.Technician only records the assigned tech on the WO header, not all techs who clocked labor
fixes.root_cause: get_technician_count() was querying COUNT(DISTINCT WO.Technician) — this only counts the tech assigned to the WO header. Multi-tech jobs where multiple people logged labor via WOLabor only show one name in WO.Technician. Also filtered on OpenDate only, missing WOs opened in prior periods.
fixes.solution: Changed query to COUNT(DISTINCT WOLabor.MechanicName) with INNER JOIN to WO. Added STRING_AGG to also return the list of tech names. Added DeletionTime IS NULL filter. Frontend now shows a 'View list' expandable section under the count so John can verify all 20 techs are being counted.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — WO.Technician vs WOLabor.MechanicName distinction is universal
fixes.lessons_learned: CRITICAL: WO.Technician is the ASSIGNED tech on the work order header — it is NOT a count of everyone who worked on the job. For accurate technician headcount, ALWAYS use COUNT(DISTINCT WOLabor.MechanicName). The WOLabor table records every tech who clocked time on a WO, regardless of who is listed as the primary technician on the WO header.

fixes.issue_summary: Customer Profitability showing non-service customers (e.g. Thomas Steel THO002) and internal billing accounts slipping through the exclusion filter
fixes.root_cause: Two issues: (1) The excluded_bill_to_customers filter was only checking i.ShipTo NOT IN (...) but internal accounts can appear in EITHER ShipTo or BillTo. (2) The dynamic service dept lookup using LIKE '%maintenance%' may match departments beyond pure service at some tenants.
fixes.solution: Fixed excluded_bill_to_customers filter to check BOTH i.ShipTo NOT IN (...) AND i.BillTo NOT IN (...) for invoices, and wo.ShipTo NOT IN (...) AND wo.Customer NOT IN (...) for WOs. This ensures internal billing accounts are excluded regardless of which field they appear in.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs that use excluded_bill_to_customers in org settings
fixes.lessons_learned: When filtering out internal/intercompany accounts in InvoiceReg queries, ALWAYS exclude on BOTH ShipTo AND BillTo fields. Internal accounts can appear in either field depending on how the WO/invoice was created. The same applies to WO queries: filter both wo.ShipTo and wo.Customer.

fixes.issue_summary: John needs to investigate why QUA001 (Quaker City Castings) shows $21,056 in costs vs $11,085 revenue — needs WO-level parts cost breakdown
fixes.root_cause: Feature request — no drill-down existed on Customer Profitability to see individual WOs and parts lines.
fixes.solution: Added new backend endpoint GET /api/reports/departments/customer-profitability/wo-detail?customer_number=QUA001&department=service. Returns all WOs for the customer with labor/parts/misc cost breakdown, plus individual WOParts line items (PartNo, Description, Qty, Cost, Sell, extended amounts). Frontend adds a 'WOs' button on each customer row that opens a modal with: (1) summary tiles (WO count, labor cost, parts cost, total cost), (2) expandable WO rows showing each WO's costs, (3) expandable parts lines within each WO showing individual part numbers and costs.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — drill-down is available universally
fixes.lessons_learned: WOParts.Cost is the STANDARD COST at the time the part was added to the WO — it may differ from the actual purchase cost if standard costs are stale in Softbase. When investigating high parts costs, always compare WOParts.Cost vs WOParts.Sell to see if the markup is appropriate, and check if standard costs have been updated recently. Also note: WOParts.Qty * WOParts.Cost = extended cost (not just WOParts.Cost alone).

fixes.issue_summary: Recurring pattern: WO.Technician undercounts active technicians — use WOLabor.MechanicName instead
fixes.root_cause: WO.Technician is a single field on the WO header. It only records the primary assigned technician. Multi-tech jobs, helper techs, and techs who clocked time but weren't the primary assignee are invisible in WO.Technician.
fixes.solution: ALWAYS use COUNT(DISTINCT WOLabor.MechanicName) for technician headcount. Join WOLabor to WO for date filtering. Add DeletionTime IS NULL to WO filter.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: CRITICAL — affects any report that counts active technicians
fixes.lessons_learned: GOLDEN RULE for technician counts: Use WOLabor.MechanicName, NOT WO.Technician. WO.Technician = primary assigned tech (1 per WO). WOLabor.MechanicName = every tech who clocked time (N per WO). For per-tech metrics (revenue/tech, hours/tech), always use the WOLabor-based count as the denominator.

fixes.issue_summary: Service department filter was too broad - %maintenance% keyword matched AMI/Alside (Dept 75) and fallback range 40-75 included Rental, Lease, Demo, AMI
fixes.root_cause: The Dept table title lookup used LIKE '%maintenance%' without excluding known non-service depts. AMI Guaranteed Maintenance program has 'maintenance' in its title. Fallback filter was AND (i.SaleDept >= 40 AND i.SaleDept <= 75) which included Rental (60), Lease (70), Demo (74), AMI (75), Misc (80).
fixes.solution: Added AND Dept NOT IN (60, 70, 74, 75, 80) to the Dept table lookup query. Changed fallback from >= 40 AND <= 75 to IN (40, 45, 47). Added logger.info to log which dept IDs are actually matched.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Medium - other tenants may also have non-service depts in the 40-75 range. The NOT IN exclusion list is IPS-specific but the fallback fix is universal.
fixes.lessons_learned: When using LIKE keyword matching on Dept.Title to identify service departments, always exclude known non-service dept codes explicitly. The fallback filter should use IN (specific codes) not a range, as ranges are too broad.

fixes.issue_summary: John asked whether service COGS includes parts costs and whether van maintenance/leases are included. The KPI tooltip was unclear.
fixes.root_cause: KPI help text for Total Service Revenue and Total Service GP did not mention parts. Prior email incorrectly stated 'service revenue does not include parts'.
fixes.solution: Updated KPI_HELP tooltips in CurrieService.jsx to explicitly state: (1) Parts ARE included in both revenue and COGS - fully-loaded Currie model. (2) Van Maintenance (5175701/5175702) and Van Leases/Internal Rental to Service (5192701/5192702) are in service COGS. (3) Both sides include parts so GP% is valid.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Low - tooltip text only, no data logic change
fixes.lessons_learned: IPS Service COGS is a fully-loaded Currie model: labor + parts cost + van maintenance + van leases + nonbillable + warranty + rework. Parts revenue and parts COGS are BOTH included. GP% is valid and reflects the full service profit margin.

fixes.issue_summary: John reported QUA001 (Quaker City Castings) shows high parts cost which is much higher than what appears on their invoices in Softbase.
fixes.root_cause: WOParts.Cost is the STANDARD COST from Softbase's item master at the time the part was added to the WO - not the actual purchase price. If standard costs are stale or incorrectly set up in Softbase, the report shows inflated parts costs that don't match invoices.
fixes.solution: (1) Added prominent yellow warning banner to Customer Profitability Report Notes section explaining the standard cost issue. (2) Updated notes.costs text to include the standard cost caveat. (3) WO drill-down (TKT-0030) allows John to see exactly which parts on which WOs are driving the cost so he can verify standard cost rates in Softbase.
fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Medium - standard cost vs. actual cost discrepancy is a universal Softbase issue. Warning applies to all tenants.
fixes.lessons_learned: WOParts.Cost = standard cost from item master, NOT actual purchase price. Always caveat parts cost figures with this note. If a customer shows unexpectedly high parts costs, the first step is to check the standard cost rates in Softbase for the parts used on their WOs.

fixes.title: IPS110/IPS130 internal accounts excluded from Customer Profitability
fixes.problem: IPS110 (New EQ Internal) and IPS130 (Used EQ Internal) were appearing in the Service Customer Profitability report as Critical customers with large losses (-$176K and -$19K). These are IPS own-fleet maintenance accounts, not real customers.
fixes.root_cause: The excluded_bill_to_customers filter was inside an if-user.organization.settings block that only ran when org settings were explicitly configured. IPS had no settings configured, so the block was skipped entirely and no exclusion was applied.
fixes.fix: Moved the hardcoded schema-specific exclusion logic (IPS110, IPS130, IPS140, IPS145, IPC140, IPC145) OUTSIDE the org settings if-block so it always runs regardless of org settings configuration. Also added RTRIM() wrapper on ShipTo/BillTo columns to handle CHAR(n) space-padding in Azure SQL Server.
fixes.lesson: Always place schema-specific hardcoded exclusions outside conditional org-settings blocks. Use RTRIM() when comparing against CHAR(n) fixed-width columns in Azure SQL Server.

fixes.title: PATTERN: Always use RTRIM() when filtering CHAR(n) columns in Azure SQL Server
fixes.problem: Azure SQL Server CHAR(n) fixed-width columns store values space-padded to the column width. e.g., CHAR(10) column storing IPS110 actually contains IPS110    (4 trailing spaces). SQL Server NOT IN comparison does NOT strip trailing spaces, so NOT IN ('IPS110') will NOT match 'IPS110    '.
fixes.root_cause: CHAR vs VARCHAR column type difference. CHAR is fixed-width and pads with spaces. VARCHAR is variable-width and does not pad.
fixes.fix: Wrap CHAR columns in RTRIM() when using IN/NOT IN filters: RTRIM(i.ShipTo) NOT IN ('IPS110','IPS130'). Known CHAR columns in Softbase Evolution: InvoiceReg.ShipTo, InvoiceReg.BillTo, WO.ShipTo, WO.BillTo, Customer.Number.
fixes.lesson: When a NOT IN filter appears to be correctly built but is not working, check if the column is CHAR(n) type and add RTRIM().

additional_fixes.issue_summary: Rental Fleet Metrics section blank on Currie Service page
additional_fixes.root_cause: get_rental_fleet_metrics() used PostDate as the date filter column in two GLDetail queries. PostDate does not exist in the IPS Azure SQL schema. The correct column is EffectiveDate, which is used universally across all other GLDetail queries in the codebase.
additional_fixes.solution: Replaced PostDate with EffectiveDate in both the rental_rev_query and deprec_query inside get_rental_fleet_metrics() in currie_report.py.
additional_fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects any org using Currie Service page — both ben002 and ind004 use EffectiveDate
additional_fixes.lessons_learned: GOLDEN RULE: GLDetail date column is ALWAYS EffectiveDate. Never use PostDate, TransDate, or Date. This applies to both ben002 (Currie) and ind004 (IPS) schemas.

additional_fixes.issue_summary: Noisy ERROR logs on every Railway deploy: deadlock on ALTER TABLE forecast_history ADD COLUMN
additional_fixes.root_cause: All 4 Gunicorn workers boot simultaneously and all call create_tables() which runs ALTER TABLE DDL. Two workers deadlock trying to acquire AccessExclusiveLock on the same table. The migration still succeeds on one worker but leaves ERROR noise in logs.
additional_fixes.solution: Added pg_try_advisory_lock(987654321) at the start of create_tables(). Only the worker that acquires the lock runs DDL; others log a skip message and return True immediately. Advisory lock is automatically released when the connection closes.
additional_fixes.multi_tenant_impact: Affects all orgs — this is a startup-time issue on Railway with 4 Gunicorn workers
additional_fixes.lessons_learned: When running DDL migrations at startup in a multi-worker environment, always use an advisory lock to serialize the migration. pg_try_advisory_lock() is non-blocking — it returns false immediately if another worker holds the lock, which is exactly what we want.

additional_fixes.issue_summary: Azure SQL does not support STRING_AGG(DISTINCT ...) syntax
additional_fixes.root_cause: Azure SQL Server (and SQL Server in general) does not allow DISTINCT inside STRING_AGG(). Attempting STRING_AGG(DISTINCT col, ', ') raises error 102: Incorrect syntax near ','.
additional_fixes.solution: Use a subquery to deduplicate first, then apply STRING_AGG on the already-distinct result: SELECT STRING_AGG(col, ', ') FROM (SELECT DISTINCT col FROM ...) AS distinct_vals
additional_fixes.multi_tenant_impact: CRITICAL — affects any query using STRING_AGG(DISTINCT) on Azure SQL
additional_fixes.lessons_learned: GOLDEN RULE for Azure SQL aggregation: STRING_AGG does NOT support DISTINCT. Always wrap in a subquery: SELECT STRING_AGG(x, ', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY x) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT x FROM table WHERE ...) AS d
